declared_release.relationship_to_paper: Initial public PaperBench release. It predates immutable arXiv v3 (2025-04-07) and lacks the stochastic-interpolants task files even though v3 lists that paper among the 20 benchmark papers.

review_time_snapshot.relationship_to_paper: Current main HEAD observed during review, more than one year after v3; it is not treated as paper-time implementation evidence.

rubric_audit_review_time_snapshot.initial_to_review_time_semantic_drift: Nineteen shared main-split rubrics retained node IDs, requirements, weights, and task categories except bridging-data-gaps, where three duplicated criteria changed from Code Development to Code Execution with corresponding wording changes. stochastic-interpolants was absent at the initial release and added on 2025-04-08. Later broad JSON diffs largely add schema fields rather than changing requirement semantics. A paper addendum received an SVD erratum on 2025-05-20.

judgeeval_audit.replay_interpretation: The released human-label trees are internally consistent with recursive sibling-weighted aggregation. This replays the deterministic aggregation layer only; raw model-judge predictions used for the paper tables are gitignored and absent, so the reported o3-mini F1 cannot be independently recomputed from this snapshot.
judgeeval_audit.aggregation_implementation: The released run_judge_eval.py pools valid leaf predictions across all five examples and computes class-macro precision/recall/F1 over that pooled vector. It does not compute an equal-paper macro average. Therefore the 1,963-leaf PINN example has far more influence than the 79-leaf semantic-self-consistency example.
judgeeval_audit.invalid_policy: Leaf grading exceptions become score 0 with valid_score=false. Parent scores still aggregate those zeros. JudgeOutput.success is true whenever at least one leaf is valid, so a partially invalid grade can remain a scored successful judge run; invalid-leaf counts are recorded but no paper result inventory was released to quantify this in reported tables.

release_inspection_limits.lfs: Git archives preserve Git-LFS pointer objects. Current sparse checkout exposed rubrics, expected-result JSON, source, tests, and aggregate tables, but large paper/submission/result binaries represented by LFS pointers were not all hydrated.
release_inspection_limits.paper_results: Aggregate and per-paper tables are released, but the complete 646 agent run records, executed submissions, judge leaf predictions, disqualification adjudications, and human time-series artifacts are not present in the audited subtree.
release_inspection_limits.replay_scope: Only deterministic rubric counts, duplicate-ID checks, and recursive score propagation were replayed locally. No paid model judge or 12-hour GPU reproduction was run.
